title: GWS Maintenance Task
description: Defines a sequential maintenance task with interactive steps for the GWS server maintenance assistant.

task-version.description: Schema version this file was authored against (semver). The runner uses the major component to select the correct schema file. See schema/VERSIONING.md.

steps.description: Ordered list of steps. Executed top-to-bottom unless a choice uses skip_steps.

StepId.description: snake_case identifier. Must be unique within the task file.

id.description: Unique task identifier. Referenced in logs and navigation.

order.description: Position in the task list. Controls run order and menu display. Must be unique across task files.

name.description: Short human-readable label shown in menus and headings.

description.description: Longer overview shown on the task start screen.

Step.description: A single interactive step within a task.

id.description: Unique step id within this task. Referenced by skip_steps and depends_on.

name.description: Short label for the step. Used in logs and progress indicators.

type.description: manual: operator acts externally then confirms via choices. run: script executes command when operator picks outcome:run. info: command runs automatically after a keypress and output is displayed for the operator to read or copy.

title.description: Screen heading override. Defaults to name if omitted.

description.description: Instructional body text displayed on the step screen. Use YAML block scalars (|) for multi-line.

command.description: Shell command to execute. Required for type:run and type:info. For type:run, fires when the operator picks a choice with outcome:run. For type:info, fires automatically after the operator presses a key.

log_to.description: Relative path under logDir to append command output to. Supports the {maintenance_start_time} placeholder. Only meaningful for type:run.

confirm.description: Question displayed directly above the numbered choices.

depends_on.description: Id of another step in this task. If that step was skipped (not run), this step is auto-skipped without prompting. Referential integrity is validated at runtime.

choices.description: Numbered options shown to the operator. For type:run steps, exactly one choice must carry outcome:run (runtime-validated). At most one choice per step should carry recommended:true.

post_run.description: Follow-up prompts shown after the step command completes. Only valid for type:run — a runtime error is raised on other types. Prompts are shown in order; all must be resolved before the step advances.

StepChoice.description: A numbered option presented to the operator on a step screen.

label.description: Text shown next to the option number, e.g. 'yes', 'no (skip)', 'no (after reboot)'.

outcome.description: advance: mark step done and proceed to the next. retry: redisplay this step — use when the operator must act first before continuing. run: execute the step command then show post_run prompts if any, then advance. outcome:run is only valid on type:run steps (runtime-validated).

recommended.description: Marks this as the preferred option. Renders a [Y/n] hint next to the confirm question. Empty input from the operator selects this choice. At most one choice per step should set this to true.

skip_steps.description: List of step ids in this task to auto-skip when this choice is taken. Used for the 'no need' pattern — e.g. an operator confirming no backup was taken should skip the later backup verification step. Referential integrity is validated at runtime.

PostRunPrompt.description: A follow-up prompt shown after a type:run step command completes. Use this for decisions tightly coupled to the execution just completed — e.g. 'Reboot now?' after apt-get upgrade, or 'Disable root shell?' after changing the root password. For decisions that are not tightly coupled, prefer a separate step with depends_on.

choices.description: Options for this follow-up prompt. Minimum two: at least one path forward and one alternative.

PostRunChoice.description: An option within a post_run prompt. Unlike step choices, post_run choices run a secondary command and/or navigate, and always advance unless outcome is reboot.

label.description: Text shown next to the option number.

recommended.description: Marks this as the preferred option. See StepChoice.recommended.

command.description: Shell command to run when this option is selected. Omit for navigation-only choices.

log.description: Log entry written when this option is selected.

outcome.description: Navigation outcome after this choice resolves. Defaults to advance if omitted.
